creator: dfilter@FreeBSD.ORG
text:
Author: brueffer Date: Thu Jun 28 10:38:59 2012 New Revision: 39147 URL: http://svn.freebsd.org/changeset/doc/39147 Log: Update for GSoC 2012. This could still use a bit more up to date information, but at least this is a first step. PR: 169502 Submitted by: Bryan Drewery Modified: head/en_US.ISO8859-1/htdocs/projects/summerofcode.xsl Modified: head/en_US.ISO8859-1/htdocs/projects/summerofcode.xsl ============================================================================== --- head/en_US.ISO8859-1/htdocs/projects/summerofcode.xsl Thu Jun 28 10:38:52 2012 (r39146) +++ head/en_US.ISO8859-1/htdocs/projects/summerofcode.xsl Thu Jun 28 10:38:59 2012 (r39147) @@ -40,8 +40,8 @@

The FreeBSD Project is looking forward to participating as a mentoring organization in Google - Summer of Code 2011. + href="http://www.google-melange.com/gsoc/homepage/google/gsoc2012">Google + Summer of Code 2012. This program funds students to contribute to an open source project over the summer break. We have had over 100 successful @@ -51,10 +51,11 @@ href="&base;/projects/summerofcode-2007.html">2007, 2008, 2009, + 2010 and - 2010.

+ 2011.

-

This page will be updated throughout the GSoC 2011 application period +

This page will be updated throughout the application period to include new information, such as project ideas, proposal information, and potential mentor contact information. If you don't see an idea that interests you, visit again in a couple of days!

@@ -99,6 +100,8 @@

For a complete list of student projects from previous years, visit:

    +
  • Summer of + Code 2011 FreeBSD Projects Wiki
  • Summer of Code 2010 FreeBSD Projects Wiki
  • Summer of @@ -272,31 +275,31 @@ href="http://wiki.freebsd.org/moin.cgi/S interest relating to the application process:

      -

    • 18 March - Google to announce what open source - organizations will participate in Google Summer of Code 2011.

      • +

    • 16 March - Google to announce what open source + organizations will participate in Google Summer of Code 2012.

      • -

    • 28 March - Student application period opens. +

    • 26 March - Student application period opens.

      • -

    • 8 April - Student application period closes. +

    • 6 April - Student application period closes.

      • -

    • 22 April - Organizations finish reviewing +

    • 20 April - Organizations finish reviewing applications an mentors registered.

      • -

    • 25 April - Accepted students announced.

      +

    • 23 April - Accepted students announced.

    Note that these dates may change, and the Google FAQ timeline is the authoritative source of detailed schedule information:

    -

      GSoC 2011 Timeline

    +

      GSoC 2012 Timeline

    All students must register with, and submit applications via, the Google Summer of Code home page:

    -

      Google Summer of Code Home Page

    • +

      Google Summer of Code Home Page

  • What advice do you have for a student who might want to submit a proposal?
